rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical regioselective 1,2-aminoselenation and oxyselenation of alkenes (arylalkenes, 1-aryl-1,3-dienes, and non-activated aliphatic alkenes) using diselenides with N–H nucleophiles (e.g., saccharin) or carboxylic acids in an undivided cell with C anode / Ni cathode.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the diaryl diselenide (R'SeSeR') generates a diselenide radical cation that dissociates into an electrophilic selenium cation (R'Se+) and a selenium radical (R'Se•); cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: β-selenyl carbocation E (from NuH addition / ring opening)
β-selenyl carbocation I (from anodic oxidation of H)

electron_flow: Path I (favored, ionic): anode oxidizes R'SeSeR' to its radical cation, which fragments to R'Se+ and R'Se•; R'Se+ adds to the alkene to give a cyclic seleniranium ion that is opened by the N- or O-nucleophile with deprotonation to deliver the 1,2-difunctionalized product, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Ni cathode. Path II (radical, not excluded): R'Se• adds to the alkene to give a β-selenyl C-radical, which is anodically oxidized to the corresponding carbocation and then trapped by the nucleophile.

probe_reagents.name: single-crystal X-ray of products 5y and 7m / 4d'
probe_reagents.observation: X-ray structures confirmed regio- and stereochemistry of the 1,2-aminoselenation/oxyselenation products

notes: Authors propose two concurrent pathways: a favored ionic seleniranium pathway (Path I) and a non-excluded radical pathway (Path II) via R'Se•-addition followed by anodic oxidation of the C-radical to a cation. Mechanistic probe experiment details are relegated to the ESI and not shown explicitly on the main-text pages; no TEMPO/BHT trapping, KIE, CV, EPR, or DFT data are presented here. Reaction tolerates strongly deactivated substrates (e.g., -NO2) and works on non-activated aliphatic alkenes; scaled to gram scale and applied to late-stage modification of bioactive molecules (estrone, probenecid, ibuprofen, ciprofibrate, etc.). Conditions: C(+)/Ni(−), Et4NBr in CH3CN, 10 mA, r.t., 2 h, undivided cell.
